discovery.run_summary: Five names report in T+3 to T+6. The 8-K bundles all fell back to cover documents (no Exhibit 99.1 financials/guidance text), so direction must come from 10-K/10-Q language and disclosed numbers. MDB shows the cleanest readable signal (Atlas reacceleration to 73% of revenue, FY26 NRR 121%, large net loss improvement, but CEO transition mid-Q4); GTLB pre-announced an in-line Q1 print and RIF, and ESTC's FY25 numbers are growth-decelerating. Most names skew RATIONAL/UNCLEAR given the press-release blackout.

discoveries.name: MongoDB
discoveries.filings_evidence: FY26 10-K (filed 2026-03-11) discloses Atlas at 73% of revenue (vs. 70% FY25, 66% FY24) — re-acceleration of the consumption mix; Q4 net ARR expansion of 121%; FY26 net loss of $71.2M vs. $129.1M FY25 vs. $176.6M FY24 — a clear two-year improvement. Voyage AI acquisition (Feb 2025) embeds embeddings/reranking inside Atlas. Q3 10-Q risk factor explicitly flagged 'slower than historical growth of our existing Atlas applications for the nine months ended October 31, 2025' — that language did not appear refreshed/escalated in the FY26 10-K Atlas risk paragraph, suggesting the headwind stabilized.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Cannot evaluate — all four earnings 8-Ks fell back to cover documents; Exhibit 99.1 press releases with prepared financials/guidance are not in the bundle. CEO Ittycheria resigned Nov 2025, replaced by CJ Desai effective Nov 9, 2025 — Q1 FY27 (reporting May 28) is Desai's first full quarter, which often comes with conservative framing.
discoveries.setup: Q1 FY27 print 3 trading days out; first full quarter under new CEO CJ Desai; Atlas mix at multi-year high and net loss compressing sharply.
discoveries.thesis: Atlas mix expansion (66%→70%→73% of revenue over three years) and a sharp net-loss reduction in FY26 ($71M vs $129M) suggest underlying durability the market may be under-crediting given the CEO transition overhang. The Q3 10-Q's 'slower than historical' Atlas language was not escalated into a new/refreshed risk factor in the FY26 10-K, which is a small derisking tell.
discoveries.what_confirms: Atlas growth re-accelerating in the Q1 press release; net ARR expansion stable or up vs the 121% Q4 print; commentary that the macro Atlas optimization headwind cited in Q3 has stabilized; conservative guide framing typical of new-CEO first quarter.
discoveries.what_kills: Desai using his first print to reset/kitchen-sink FY27 guidance; Atlas consumption growth decelerating again or NRR falling below 120%; AI-application Atlas tailwind narrative weakening in management commentary.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a CEO transitions mid-fiscal-year, the first earnings print under the new CEO is structurally biased toward reset/sandbag — track whether the filings ahead of the print show improving underlying metrics (which give the new CEO room to guide conservatively and beat) vs. deteriorating ones (which invite a kitchen-sink).
research_pointers: Check the Exhibit 99.1 Q4 FY26 press release (filed 2026-03-02) for the FY27 guide range and how conservatively Desai framed it
Atlas consumption commentary — did the 'slower than historical' Q3 caveat persist into Q4 commentary?
Net ARR expansion rate trajectory: 121% in Q4; watch for stability
Voyage AI revenue contribution disclosure — first full year integrated
Buyside expectations into the print after CEO change — likely depressed

discoveries.name: GitLab
discoveries.filings_evidence: May 11, 2026 8-K (T-15 days from print) simultaneously (a) reaffirmed Q1 FY27 in-line with March 3 guidance and full-year FY27 guidance, and (b) announced a reduction in force. RIFs on the eve of a print are rarely a sign of upside — management is realigning cost structure to defend operating margin while signaling that growth is no better than the prior guide. FY26 net loss widened to $56.0M from $6.3M in FY25 despite 26% revenue growth; FY26 NRR fell to 118% from 123% in FY25 — a clear deceleration in expansion. Q3 FY26 10-Q risk factors were updated to remove the 'relatively new' descriptor for the market, suggesting management views the market as maturing (slowing).
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Cannot evaluate guide-vs-actual from the bundle (all 8-Ks are cover documents). The May 11 pre-announcement explicitly only reaffirmed — not raised — guidance, which historically for high-multiple SaaS is a negative tell going into a print where the bar is 'beat and raise.'
discoveries.setup: Q1 FY27 print 6 trading days out; company pre-announced in-line (not above) and announced a RIF 3 weeks before the print; NRR decelerating 123%→118% YoY; net loss widening despite revenue growth.
discoveries.thesis: The combination of (1) a pre-print 'in-line only' guidance reaffirmation, (2) a same-day workforce reduction, and (3) clear deceleration in net retention (123%→118%) and widening net loss in the just-closed FY26 suggests the filings are flagging more downside risk than a SaaS multiple typically prices. RIF + reaffirm is rarely a setup for an upside surprise on a name where the bull case requires AI-driven re-acceleration.
discoveries.what_confirms: Q1 FY27 revenue/RPO printing exactly in-line with the reaffirmed range with no raise; NRR continuing to drift below 118%; soft commentary on GitLab Duo / Agent Platform monetization despite GA in January 2026; FY27 guide held flat rather than raised.
discoveries.what_kills: Q1 beat with a guidance raise (would imply the May 11 reaffirmation was a sandbag); NRR stabilizing or ticking up; explicit Duo Agent Platform credit/usage metrics that show monetization traction; the RIF framed as pure reinvestment with revenue line untouched.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a company pre-announces 'in-line' guidance simultaneously with a RIF in the two weeks before a print, treat that pairing as a directional signal — management is choosing to defend margin rather than raise the top line.
research_pointers: May 11, 2026 8-K — read the Staples letter (Exhibit 99.1) for the RIF rationale and any reinvestment framing
March 3, 2026 Q4 FY26 press release — original FY27 guide range that was reaffirmed
NRR trajectory: 123% FY25 → 118% FY26 → watch Q1
Duo Agent Platform GA was January 2026 — first quarter where AI credits could move the needle (or visibly fail to)
CFO Jessica Ross started ~Jan 15, 2026 — her first print, with a RIF; she has incentive not to raise

skipped.reason: FY25 10-K shows revenue growth decelerating (19%→17%) and Elastic Cloud at 46% of revenue — directionally cautious — but with all four earnings 8-Ks falling back to cover documents (no guidance/actuals text) and FY26 reported revenue/Cloud mix not in bundle, evidence is too thin to commit to OVERDONE; RATIONAL.

Net loss narrowing ($255M FY25 → $189M FY26) but persistent (and accumulated deficit $2.15B); CRR for Core $5K+ customers only 97% and large-customer NRR 96% — sub-100%, a structural headwind. CEO/COO/GC all transitioned in fiscal year. Buyback increased $160M. Mixed signals with no readable 8-K guidance text — UNCLEAR.

FY25 10-K discloses ~$85M tariff hit to FY25 operating income with explicit uncertainty about further tariff levels; Feb 20, 2026 Supreme Court IEEPA ruling adds policy ambiguity. Strategy is mid-execution under new CEO Super. Earnings 8-Ks are cover docs only — direction unreadable from filings alone — UNCLEAR.

discovery.no_signals_note: Earnings 8-K bundles all fell back to cover documents across every candidate, removing the prepared-financials/guidance evidence that normally drives Screen 2 calls; this materially limited confidence across the slate and pushed several otherwise-tradeable setups to RATIONAL/UNCLEAR.
